title: Separate Upstream Request Headers from Client Response Headers
prompt: Use $nextjs-secure-bff to improve this code according to api-proxy-headers.
notes: In Proxy, `request: { headers }` changes what the app receives, while response headers are visible to clients. Do not forward all incoming headers or leak cookies, tokens, or internal metadata.

title: Keep Route Handlers at the Public Boundary
prompt: Use $nextjs-secure-bff to improve this code according to api-route-boundaries.
notes: Route Handlers are public API endpoints. Validate content type, payload shape, and authorization before calling internal services. Keep public errors generic and return only fields that are part of the public contract.

title: Validate Webhook and Callback Requests Before Side Effects
prompt: Use $nextjs-secure-bff to improve this code according to api-webhook-validation.
notes: Webhook and callback endpoints need authentication before side effects. Validate signatures or secrets, constrain accepted parameters, and avoid returning sensitive failure details.

title: Isolate Data Access and Return Minimal DTOs
prompt: Use $nextjs-secure-bff to improve this code according to security-dal-dto.
notes: Use a Data Access Layer for privileged reads and writes. It should run only on the server, perform authorization, and return DTOs shaped for the UI instead of raw database records.

title: Keep Secrets Behind Server-only Modules
prompt: Use $nextjs-secure-bff to improve this code according to security-env-server-only.
notes: Private environment variables and privileged SDKs belong in server-only modules. Do not expose them through shared utilities or Client Component imports. Only `NEXT_PUBLIC_` values should be assumed client-visible.

title: Re-authorize Inside Every Server Action
prompt: Use $nextjs-secure-bff to improve this code according to security-server-action-authz.
notes: Server Actions can be invoked outside the visible page UI. Re-check authentication and authorization inside the action or in the server-only DAL function it calls. Page-level redirects do not protect the action.
